annotations.label: Ellipse (locus definition)

anchor.snippet: an ellipse is a plane curve surrounding two focal points

annotations.note: No `Ellipse` definition (as the locus where the sum of distances to two foci is constant) exists in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Eccentricity of an ellipse

anchor.snippet: The elongation of an ellipse is measured by its eccentricity

annotations.note: Mathlib4 has no notion of conic eccentricity.

annotations.label: Standard equation of an ellipse

anchor.snippet: the equation of a standard ellipse centered at the origin is

annotations.note: The standard Cartesian equation x²/a² + y²/b² = 1 is not stated in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Ellipse as conic section

anchor.snippet: Ellipses are the closed type of conic section

annotations.note: Mathlib4 has no formal theory of conic sections.

annotations.label: Focus-directrix definition of ellipse

anchor.snippet: An ellipse may also be defined in terms of one focal point and a line outside the ellipse called the directrix

annotations.note: No focus-directrix construction is present in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Center, axes, vertices, focal distance, eccentricity

anchor.snippet: The midpoint

annotations.note: These named ellipse parameters are not defined in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Circle as special ellipse

anchor.snippet: yields a circle and is included as a special type of ellipse

annotations.note: Mathlib4 has circles (`Sphere`/`Metric.sphere`) but no ellipse type subsuming them.

annotations.label: Circular directrix

anchor.snippet: is called the circular directrix

annotations.note: The circular directrix construction is absent from Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Dandelin spheres: conic section is ellipse

anchor.snippet: Using Dandelin spheres , one can prove that any section of a cone with a plane is an ellipse

annotations.note: No Dandelin-spheres argument or planar-section-of-cone theorem exists in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Standard form coordinates of ellipse

anchor.snippet: The standard form of an ellipse in Cartesian coordinates assumes that the origin is the center of the ellipse

annotations.note: Mathlib4 does not formalize the standard Cartesian form of an ellipse.

annotations.label: Derivation of standard ellipse equation

anchor.snippet: Removing the radicals by suitable squarings

annotations.note: Mathlib4 contains no derivation of the ellipse equation from the focal definition.

annotations.label: Semi-major and semi-minor axes, co-vertices

anchor.snippet: are called the semi-major and semi-minor axes

annotations.note: Semi-axes of an ellipse are not defined in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Symmetry of the ellipse

anchor.snippet: It follows from the equation that the ellipse is symmetric with respect to the coordinate axes

annotations.note: There is no statement about ellipse symmetry in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Principal axes convention

anchor.snippet: Throughout this article, the semi-major and semi-minor axes are denoted

annotations.note: Notational conventions for ellipse principal axes do not appear in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Linear eccentricity

anchor.snippet: This is the distance from the center to a focus

annotations.note: Mathlib4 has no `linearEccentricity` or comparable notion.

annotations.label: Eccentricity formula

anchor.snippet: The eccentricity can be expressed as

annotations.note: The formula e = c/a is not in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Zero-eccentricity circle

anchor.snippet: An ellipse with equal axes

annotations.note: Mathlib4 does not state the e=0 ↔ circle correspondence.

annotations.label: Latus rectum and semi-latus rectum

anchor.snippet: The length of the chord through one focus, perpendicular to the major axis, is called the latus rectum

annotations.note: No latus-rectum concept is defined in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Semi-latus rectum equals radius of curvature at vertex

anchor.snippet: is equal to the radius of curvature at the vertices

annotations.note: This curvature identity for ellipses is not formalized in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Exterior line, tangent, secant

anchor.snippet: intersects an ellipse at 0, 1, or 2 points, respectively called an exterior line , tangent and secant

annotations.note: No ellipse line-intersection classification exists in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Tangent equation at point on ellipse

anchor.snippet: Through any point of an ellipse there is a unique tangent

annotations.note: The tangent line formula xx₀/a² + yy₀/b² = 1 is not in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Conjugate diameters criterion

anchor.snippet: then the points lie on two conjugate diameters

annotations.note: Conjugate diameters are not formalized in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Shifted ellipse equation

anchor.snippet: If the standard ellipse is shifted to have center

annotations.note: The translated ellipse equation is not in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: General ellipse as quadric

anchor.snippet: the ellipse is defined as a quadric

annotations.note: Mathlib4 has no theory of quadric/conic classification giving general ellipses.

annotations.label: Non-degeneracy criterion via determinant

anchor.snippet: Then the ellipse is a non-degenerate real ellipse if and only if

annotations.note: The discriminant criterion for non-degenerate ellipses is absent from Mathlib4.

annotations.label: General-form coefficients from canonical parameters

anchor.snippet: The general equation's coefficients can be obtained from known semi-major axis

annotations.note: Conversion between canonical and general ellipse coefficients is not in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Canonical parameters from general-form coefficients

anchor.snippet: the canonical form parameters can be obtained from the general-form coefficients

annotations.note: Mathlib4 does not provide formulas extracting a, b, center, and tilt from general coefficients.

annotations.label: Standard parametric representation

anchor.snippet: a parametric representation of the standard ellipse

annotations.note: The (a cos t, b sin t) ellipse parametrization is not formalized in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Eccentric anomaly

anchor.snippet: The parameter t (called the eccentric anomaly in astronomy)

annotations.note: Eccentric anomaly is not defined in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Rational parametric equation of ellipse

anchor.snippet: and the rational parametric equation of an ellipse

annotations.note: The rational tangent-half-angle parametrization of an ellipse is not in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Projective-line rational parametrization

anchor.snippet: is considered to be a point on the real projective line

annotations.note: Mathlib4 has no rational ellipse parametrization via the projective line.

annotations.label: Tangent-slope parametric representation

anchor.snippet: A parametric representation, which uses the slope

annotations.note: The slope-parameter ellipse parametrization is not formalized in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Tangent equation via slope parameter

anchor.snippet: The equation of the tangent at point

annotations.note: Mathlib4 does not state the tangent equation in slope-parameter form.

annotations.label: Ellipse as affine image of unit circle

anchor.snippet: Any ellipse is an affine image of the unit circle

annotations.note: This affine characterization of ellipses is not in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Vertices of affine-image ellipse

anchor.snippet: The four vertices of the ellipse are

annotations.note: Formulas for the vertices of an affine-image ellipse are not in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Area from Apollonios theorem

anchor.snippet: From Apollonios theorem (see below) one obtains: The area of an ellipse

annotations.note: No statement giving ellipse area via Apollonios is in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Semiaxes from conjugate-diameter system

anchor.snippet: Solving this nonlinear system

annotations.note: Mathlib4 has no system recovering semi-axes from conjugate diameters.

annotations.label: Implicit representation via Cramer's rule

anchor.snippet: Solving the parametric representation

annotations.note: The Cramer-rule-derived implicit ellipse equation is not in Mathlib4.

anchor.snippet: Example : For the ellipse with equation

annotations.note: This worked example is not formalized in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Polar form relative to center

annotations.note: Polar form of an ellipse about its center is not in Mathlib4.

anchor.snippet: If instead we use polar coordinates with the origin at one focus

annotations.note: Polar form r = p/(1+e cos θ) is not formalized in Mathlib4.

anchor.snippet: The angle

annotations.note: The true-anomaly parameter is not defined in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Directrix of ellipse

anchor.snippet: Each of the two lines parallel to the minor axis

annotations.note: Directrix lines of an ellipse are not defined in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Focus-directrix ratio equals eccentricity

anchor.snippet: For an arbitrary point

annotations.note: The focus-directrix ratio property is not in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Converse focus-directrix definition

anchor.snippet: For any point

annotations.note: The converse focus-directrix characterization is absent from Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Conic classification by eccentricity

anchor.snippet: This is the equation of an ellipse

annotations.note: No e<1/=1/>1 conic-type classification is in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Directrix-focus inversion construction

anchor.snippet: are inverse with respect to the circle inversion

annotations.note: Mathlib4 has `EuclideanGeometry.inversion` but no statement relating ellipse focus and directrix via inversion.

annotations.label: Focus-to-focus reflection property

anchor.snippet: The normal at a point

annotations.note: The ellipse reflection property is not formalized in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Reflection of rays to second focus

anchor.snippet: The rays from one focus are reflected by the ellipse to the second focus

annotations.note: Ray-reflection between foci is not stated in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Alignment with major axis

anchor.snippet: reflected rays will eventually align closely with the major axis

annotations.note: This asymptotic statement is absent from Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Midpoints of parallel chords on a diameter (circle)

anchor.snippet: The midpoints of parallel chords lie on a diameter

annotations.note: No statement about midpoints of parallel chords lying on a diameter appears in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Conjugate diameters of an ellipse

anchor.snippet: Two diameters

annotations.note: Conjugate diameters are not defined in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Conjugate diameters via parallel tangents

anchor.snippet: of an ellipse are conjugate whenever the tangents at

anchor.snippet: For an ellipse with semi-axes

annotations.note: Apollonios's theorem on conjugate semi-diameters is not formalized in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Orthoptic / director circle

anchor.snippet: the intersection points of orthogonal tangents lie on the circle

annotations.note: The director-circle theorem is not in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: de La Hire's point construction

anchor.snippet: The following construction of single points of an ellipse is due to de La Hire

annotations.note: de La Hire's construction is not in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Pins-and-string locus method

anchor.snippet: The characterization of an ellipse as the locus of points so that sum of the distances to the foci is constant

annotations.note: The pins-and-string locus is not formalized, since the ellipse itself is not defined in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Paper strip method (first)

anchor.snippet: If the strip slides with both ends on the axes of the desired ellipse

annotations.note: The paper-strip (trammel) construction is not in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Paper strip method (second)

anchor.snippet: Then the free end of the strip traces an ellipse

annotations.note: The second paper-strip construction is not formalized in Mathlib4.

annotations.note: Radii of curvature at ellipse vertices are not in Mathlib4.

annotations.note: Steiner's projective conic generation is not formalized in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Ellipse as hypotrochoid (Tusi couple)

anchor.snippet: The ellipse is a special case of the hypotrochoid

annotations.note: Hypotrochoids and the Tusi-couple description of an ellipse are not in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Circle determined by three non-collinear points

anchor.snippet: is uniquely determined by three points

annotations.note: Mathlib4's `circumcenter`/`circumradius` of an n-simplex specialize to the unique circle through three non-collinear (affinely independent) points.

annotations.label: Inscribed angle theorem for circles

anchor.snippet: The four points are on a circle, if and only if the angles at

annotations.note: Mathlib4 proves the oriented-angle inscribed-angle/cyclic-quadrilateral identity for cospherical points (`Cospherical.two_zsmul_oangle_eq`), giving one direction of the inscribed-angle theorem.

annotations.label: Three-point form of circle equation

anchor.snippet: As a consequence, one obtains an equation for the circle determined by three non-collinear points

annotations.note: The explicit determinant/three-point equation form of a circle is not stated in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Ellipse determined by three points (family of fixed eccentricity)

anchor.snippet: such an ellipse is determined by three points not on a line

annotations.note: Determination of an axis-aligned ellipse by three points is not in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Inscribed angle theorem for ellipses

anchor.snippet: The four points are on an ellipse with equation

annotations.note: The inscribed-angle theorem for ellipses is not formalized in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Three-point form of ellipse equation

anchor.snippet: A consequence, one obtains an equation for the ellipse determined by three non-collinear points

annotations.note: The three-point form of an ellipse equation is not in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Pole-polar relation

anchor.snippet: Such a relation between points and lines generated by a conic is called pole-polar relation or polarity

annotations.note: Conic pole-polar duality is not defined in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Properties of pole-polar relation

anchor.snippet: By calculation one can confirm the following properties of the pole-polar relation

annotations.note: Mathlib4 contains no pole-polar property lemmas.

annotations.label: Intersection of polars is pole of line through poles

anchor.snippet: The intersection point of two polars is the pole of the line through their poles

annotations.note: This duality statement is absent from Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Foci and directrices as pole-polar pairs

anchor.snippet: belong to pairs of pole and polar

annotations.note: Identification of foci/directrices as pole-polar pairs is not in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Area of tilted ellipse

anchor.snippet: In this case a simple formula still applies

annotations.note: Area formula for a tilted/parametric ellipse is not in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Kepler's first law

anchor.snippet: Johannes Kepler discovered that the orbits along which the planets travel around the Sun are ellipses
